text.title: PMBoK 8 Six Core Principles
text.summary: Six principles that anchor every PM decision; each paired with a concrete measurable indicator.

list.item: Adopt Holistic View — see the project as part of a larger system; consider upstream and downstream effects on organisation and stakeholders. Indicator: context section in charter referencing organisational goals.
Focus on Value — deliver outcomes and benefits, not just outputs. Indicator: outcomes table mapping each deliverable to a named stakeholder benefit.
Embed Quality — quality is built into process and definition of done, not inspected in at the end. Indicator: DoD / acceptance criteria present before work starts.
Lead Accountably — ethical, transparent leadership with clear escalation paths. Indicator: decision log with rationale and RACI with named accountability per deliverable.

Integrate Sustainability — consider environmental, social, and governance impact throughout. Indicator: at least one measurable sustainability proxy (CO2 estimate, accessibility score, social impact metric).
Build Empowered Teams — trust, autonomy, psychological safety, and distributed leadership. Indicator: team working agreements exist and team charter was co-created with the team.

section.title: PMBoK 7 to PMBoK 8 Consolidation
section.p: PMBoK 7 had 12 principles; PMBoK 8 reduces to 6. Key collapses: "Stewardship" + "Leadership" + "Ethics" → Lead Accountably; "Systems Thinking" + "Complexity" → Adopt Holistic View; "Team" + "Leadership" → Build Empowered Teams. The PMBoK 7 "Quality" principle becomes Embed Quality. Sustainability is new and explicit.

section.title: Decision Framework
section.p: Apply at every significant PM decision point. Ask in order:

list.item: Does this deliver value? (stakeholder benefit, not just task completion)
Does this consider the whole system? (upstream/downstream effects)
Is quality embedded? (DoD, acceptance criteria, not post-hoc testing)
Am I leading accountably? (transparent rationale, named accountability)
Is it sustainable? (at least one numeric proxy)
Does it empower the team? (autonomy, not control; working agreements honoured)
